description: Mapping of PDPA 2010 (Act 709) sections to Codes of Practice issued under s57 of the Act

codes.title: Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (General) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Banking and Financial Institutions) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Communications) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Insurance and Takaful) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Health) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Aviation) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Utilities — Electricity) 2022
Personal Data Protection Code of Practice (Utilities — Water) 2022

mappings.sectionTitle: Application of Personal Data Protection Principles

codeReferences.notes: The General Code provides comprehensive guidance on applying all seven principles across all sectors. Sector-specific codes supplement the General Code.
The General Code provides the framework for implementing the General Principle, including guidance on lawful basis assessment and consent management.
Banking sector guidance on lawful bases for customer data processing, including product offerings, credit assessment, and regulatory reporting.
Healthcare guidance on lawful bases for patient data processing, including treatment, billing, research, and public health.
The General Code specifies the content, format, and delivery mechanism for data protection notices across all sectors.
Sector-specific guidance for telcos and ISPs on subscriber notices, including digital delivery and layered notice approaches.
General guidance on managing disclosures to third parties, including data processors, service providers, and regulatory bodies.
Banking-specific guidance on credit bureau reporting, Central Credit Reference Information System (CCRIS), and inter-bank disclosures.
The General Code provides a baseline security framework applicable to all data controllers, cross-referenced to the Security Standards 2015.
Enhanced security requirements for banking sector, including multi-factor authentication, encryption standards, and network security for online banking.
Healthcare sector security requirements for electronic health records (EHR), medical imaging systems, and clinical information systems.
Communications sector security for customer data, call records, and network management data.
Insurance sector security for sensitive underwriting data, claims data, and health-related insurance data.
Electricity utility sector security for smart meter data, consumption data, and customer account information.
Water utility sector security requirements for customer billing data and water consumption records.
Aviation sector security for passenger name records (PNR), passport data, and frequent flyer information.
The General Code provides a general framework for data retention schedules and secure deletion procedures, supplemented by the Retention Standard 2015.

Banking sector retention schedule specifying minimum and maximum retention periods for customer due diligence records, transaction records, and credit files, which must comply with Bank Negara Malaysia requirements.
Healthcare sector retention periods for medical records, prescription records, and clinical trial data, informed by Ministry of Health regulations.
The General Code sets out data quality and accuracy requirements cross-referenced to the Data Integrity Standard 2015.
The General Code provides procedures for handling access requests, including prescribed forms, response timelines, and permissible grounds for refusal.
Enhanced obligations for processing sensitive personal data, including requirements for additional consent mechanisms, enhanced security measures, and limited access controls.
Comprehensive sector-specific guidance for health data, which constitutes sensitive personal data under the Act, addressing clinical use, research, insurance, and secondary uses.
Insurance-specific handling of health and medical data obtained during underwriting and claims assessment.
